title: brachyglottis brunonis
intro: Brachyglottis brunonis, also known as the bronze fern, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand, characterized by its distinctive bronze-green leaves. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Brachyglottis brunonis include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
